title: brossaea insipida
intro: Brossaea insipida, also known as the bitter cassava or manioc, is a plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its bitter-tasting roots and leaves. The medicinal properties of Brossaea insipida lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever, making it a traditional remedy for various health issues. Its health benefits include helping to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. In terms of herbal preparations, Brossaea insipida can be consumed as a tea, taken in capsule form, or applied topically as a paste to alleviate skin irritations.
